summary:
The central region of intrafusal fibers lacks myofibrils and cannot contract, while the terminal portions are capable of contraction under gamma motor neuron activation. This makes the ends the sole contractile component of intrafusal fibers.

\boxed{\text{Ends}}

- What part contracts?
- Why?
